title: Account Lifecycle — New AD Users with Immediate CDE Group Assignment

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

dataSources: `index=windows` EventCode 4720 (user created), 4728 (member added to security-enabled global group)
spl:
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4720,4728) earliest=-24h
| transaction TargetUserName maxspan=60m startswith=EventCode=4720 endswith=EventCode=4728
| lookup pci_cde_ad_groups.csv GroupName OUTPUT cde_group
| where cde_group="true"
| table duration, TargetUserName, GroupName, SubjectUserName
value: Detects user creations followed within a short window by addition to CDE-privileged groups so provisioning follows lifecycle controls and management approval for access to account data.
implementation: (1) Map `TargetUserName` alignment between events—may require `rename` on 4720 field `TargetUserName` as new user and 4728 `MemberName`. (2) Adjust `transaction` keys per environment testing. (3) Alert IAM for out-of-band adds. (4) Evidence for user identification procedures.
visualization: Timeline (transactions), table (new users to CDE groups), single value (count per day).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
